panels.title: Welcome to SpinoVerse XR project.
panels.description:
In this module, you’re going to experience Spine Core and its functions in Mixed Reality.

Select Start to enter the experiment.

panels.title: Introduction
panels.description:
The Spinal core is one of the most important parts, providing the main support, allowing you to stand upright, bend and twist.

Click Next to Continue.

panels.title: Spine Parts
panels.description:
There are a total of 31 pairs of nerves connected to your body.

1. 8 cervical nerves (C1–C8)
2. 12 thoracic nerves (T1–T12)
3. 5 lumbar nerves (L1–L5)
4. 5 sacral nerves (S1–S5)
5. 1 coccygeal nerve (Co1)

Select Next to Continue.

panels.title: Cervical Spine
panels.description:
The Cervical spine, or neck region, consists of seven stacked vertebrae (C1-C7) connecting the skull to the thoracic spine, designed for flexibility, supporting the head, and protecting the upper spinal cord.

Select Next to Continue.

panels.title: Thoracic Spine
panels.description:
The Thoracic spine (mid-back) is the longest, most rigid section of the vertebral column, consisting of 12 vertebrae (T1–T12) located between the cervical and lumbar spine.

Click Next to Continue.

panels.title: Lumbar Spine
panels.description:
The Lumbar spine, or lower back, consists of five durable vertebrae (L1–L5) located between the thoracic spine and the sacrum, designed to support upper body weight, absorb shock, and facilitate movement.

Click Next to Continue.
